title: Medscape 'Inside the Quest to Make Old Cells Young Again' synthesizes the post-Life Bio reprogramming era
description: A major Medscape feature pulled the field together this week: Verdin (Buck) on metabolic fidelity, Life Biosciences' $80M raise and first patient dose, Texas A&M's nasal spray that calmed neuroinflammaging and restored youthful brain mitochondria, and Wyss-Coray's microfluidic chip data showing healthy human fat/liver tissue aging in just 4 days when exposed to old donor serum. Together these threads frame the first practitioner-aimed canonical narrative of clinical-stage rejuvenation.

title: Texas A&M nasal spray calms neuroinflammaging and restores youthful brain mitochondria
description: Medscape highlighted a Texas A&M research program in which a nasal spray formulation reduced neuroinflammaging and reversed mitochondrial dysfunction in the aged brain. Combined with Wyss-Coray's plasma exchange data, this points to a non-injectable, non-surgical route for brain-age intervention that could leapfrog the field's current invasive delivery constraints.

title: Wyss-Coray lab Nature Bioengineering paper: old human serum ages tissue chips in 4 days
description: The Wyss-Coray lab's March 2026 Nature Biomedical Engineering paper, profiled this week, showed that exposing healthy human fat and liver tissue grown on microfluidic chips to serum from older donors produced measurable cellular aging in 4 days. This visceral, fast-readout assay accelerates therapeutic screening and reinforces blood-borne age-promoting factors as a tractable druggable target.

title: Amedea Pharma 5th Medical Innovation Olympics — Longevity Medicine theme launches at Rockefeller Center
description: The 5th annual Medical Innovation Olympics opens at Rockefeller Center June 12 with the 2026 theme 'Crystal Clear, Treatment Near' explicitly centered on Longevity Medicine. Competing startups, pharma, biotech and academic teams will pitch concepts and products to angel, strategic and VC investors at one of Fierce Pharma's Top 4 innovation challenges, signaling longevity's mainstream investor traction.

title: Atrogi GRK-biased β-agonist advances to Phase 2 — Karolinska metabolic boost without muscle wasting
description: Karolinska Institute spin-out Atrogi AB, backed by the Nova Nordisk Foundation, advanced its GRK-biased adrenergic agonist (published in Cell 2025) into Phase 2 — designed to mimic GLP-1 metabolic benefits without appetite suppression, muscle wasting, or GI side effects. If translation holds, it becomes a structurally important next-generation companion to the Ozempic-class for longevity protocols.

title: Inside the Quest to Make Old Cells Young Again
description: Major Medscape feature covering Life Biosciences first patient dose, $80M raise, Buck Institute metabolic fidelity framing (Verdin), Wyss-Coray microfluidic chip data, and Texas A&M brain nasal spray.

title: Amedea Pharma announces return of 5th annual Medical Innovation Olympics — Longevity Medicine theme
description: Olympic Trials Qualification Session 1 opens June 12 at Rockefeller Center; theme 'Crystal Clear, Treatment Near' with Longevity Medicine taking center stage; recognized by Fierce Pharma as Top 4 innovation challenge globally.

title: Dr Mike Roizen: Longevity keeps getting closer to reality — 40 at 90?
description: Roizen rounds up the week's top longevity reads: Yamanaka-factor human trial (Life Bio) as breakthrough of the week, Verve-102 CRISPR-edited LDL (62% reduction, 88% PCSK9 protein drop, 18+ month duration in Heart-2 trial), Atrogi GRK-biased β-agonist Phase 2, GLP-1 breast cancer mortality benefit, visceral fat vs BMI study (Lancet Diabetes Endocrinology), collagen/creatine supplement evidence updates, caffeine timing for sleep.

title: Senescent fibroblasts in aging and pulmonary fibrosis — Frontiers in Aging review
description: New peer-reviewed review covers senolytics and senomorphics targeting senescent fibroblasts in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis and chronic lung aging.

title: Gowing Life: Could rapamycin slow brain aging?
description: Detailed plain-language breakdown of long-term low-dose rapamycin in mice: T-cell rebalancing, microglia inflammatory blunting after LPS challenge, no broad immunosuppression — translational implications for daily low-dose protocols in humans.

title: Scientists target NOX4 protein in skeletal muscle to combat aging
description: Monash University Science Advances paper identifies skeletal-muscle NOX4 as a master regulator of exercise-induced anti-aging response; restoring NOX4 reversed frailty muscle wasting in preclinical models; team now screening cruciferous-vegetable compounds.

title: Manulife Asia Care Survey 2026: Independence as Asia's new legacy
description: Survey of 9,000+ adults across 9 Asian markets shows respondents estimate 13-14 years of late-life care need; Manulife backs $350M Longevity Institute for extending healthy years and financial resilience.

title: FDA approves bemotrizinol — first new active sunscreen ingredient in 25+ years
description: FDA's first new active sunscreen ingredient since 2000; BEMT offers broad UVA/UVB protection used in Europe/Asia for years; major upgrade to US photoaging-prevention stack.

title: Verve-102: Phase 1b Heart-2 results — 62% LDL reduction, 88% PCSK9 protein drop, 18+ months durability
source: New England Journal of Medicine via Roizen
description: Eli Lilly-stewarded VRV-102 CRISPR/mRNA lipid nanoparticle therapy permanently edits PCSK9 in the liver; Cleveland Clinic-led Phase 1b results show large durable LDL reduction with no serious adverse events — major cardiovascular longevity datapoint.

title: Ingenix raises €13M from Sofinnova Partners-led syndicate
description: Sofinnova Partners leads €13M round for Ingenix to scale modality-fusion novel architecture; reflects ongoing private capital flow into AI-enabled biotech adjacent to longevity drug discovery.

title: Daily briefing: Trial to 'de-age' cells treats first person
description: Nature Daily Briefing names Life Biosciences' first ER-100 dose as a defining moment for partial-reprogramming therapeutics.

title: Life Biosciences (ER-100) Phase 1 trial: first patient dosed
description: NCT07290244 Phase 1 trial of OCT4/SOX2/KLF4 reprogramming gene therapy in glaucoma/NAION; under 20 patients across Boston, NYC, LA, Charleston with doxycycline on/off control.

title: Albert Einstein College of Medicine launches BIO-VITAL gerotherapeutics validation program
description: BIO-VITAL connects biotech/pharma partners with Einstein's three integrated cores (cellular hallmarks, preclinical models, human longevity multi-omics) backed by Ofer Family Foundation.

title: Rejuvenate Bio: $6M financing and strategic R&D collaboration with Merck Animal Health
description: $6M round led by VCapital with Merck Animal Health, Kendall Capital Partners, Connecticut Innovations and Digitalis; continued gene-therapy platform development.

title: UT San Antonio launches multi-phase rapamycin study in older adults
description: Dean Kellogg Jr. (UT Health San Antonio Geriatrics) leads multi-phase clinical study to understand rapamycin's biological effects in older adults.

title: 60 Minutes: Dog Aging Project rapamycin trial could unlock secrets for people
description: NIH-funded large-scale rapamycin RCT in pet dogs; hundreds dosed in active arm with healthspan endpoints; mainstream coverage builds public/regulator momentum.

title: PEARL trial: rapamycin slows muscle and bone aging in first long-term human RCT
description: 115 adults 50-85 on 10 mg/week rapamycin for 48 weeks; muscle preservation in women, bone preservation in men, no serious adverse events.

title: Stanfield-Kaeberlein rapamycin RCT: 6 mg/week sirolimus on strength and endurance
description: BMC-registered double-blind RCT in 40 adults aged 65-80 over 13 weeks; strength and endurance primary endpoints; next translational checkpoint for low-dose rapamycin.

title: Nature 2026 master review: therapeutic strategies for aging and age-associated diseases
description: Comprehensive review integrating senolytics, senomorphics, senoreversion, metabolic/dietary interventions, biomarkers, multiomics and AI into one unified geroscience framework.

title: Sinclair + Tally Health: first comprehensive evidence map of human epigenetic aging interventions
description: Frontiers in Genetics review identifies exercise, semaglutide, pitavastatin, plant-rich diet, caloric restriction, omega-3, multivitamin as interventions associated with favorable epigenetic clock changes (41 human studies).

title: UCSD: Semaglutide may slow biological aging in randomized HIV lipohypertrophy trial
description: First RCT showing semaglutide slowed DunedinPACE by 9% and PCGrimAge mortality clock relative to placebo over 32 weeks; published in Nature Communications.

title: NewLimit raises $435M Series C at $3.1B valuation
description: Founders Fund-led round (Thrive, Lilly Ventures, Friedman, Gross) brings Brian Armstrong-founded cell reprogramming company to $3.1B valuation ahead of first liver age-reversal human trial.

title: Contrary Research: The Anti-Aging Therapeutics Boom
description: Investor-facing market memo framing partial reprogramming, senolytics, GLP-1 aging trials, AI-driven drug discovery and consumer wearables as a structural investable boom.

title: Forever Healthy Foundation launches Evipedia.ai — open encyclopedia for 500+ longevity interventions
description: Michael Greve's Forever Healthy Foundation releases Evipedia.ai with in-depth evidence reviews of 500+ longevity interventions including first-generation rejuvenation therapies, supplements, botanicals, lifestyle protocols.

title: VISIONS 2026: United in Vision Schedule (Foundation Fighting Blindness + Retina International)
description: VISIONS Conference and Retina International World Congress kicks off June 12 in Boston; high-relevance event given Life Biosciences' eye-first ER-100 clinical program.
